---
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cran.rstudio.com/"))

# List of required packages
packages <- c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "caret", "randomForest", "knitr", "ggplot2", "readxl", "hms")

# Install any missing packages
to_install <- packages[!packages %in% installed.packages()]
if(length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install)

# Load all packages
lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE)

# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)  # For data manipulation and visualization
library(lubridate)  # For working with dates and times

# Simulate loading your data (replace this with the actual file path)
data <- read.csv("/Users/gyansharma/Desktop/Certification/crash_data.csv")

# Inspect the data
head(data)  # View the first few rows of the dataset
str(data)   # Check the structure of the dataset

# Clean and transform the data
crash_data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    `CRASH DATE` = mdy(`CRASH.DATE`),        # Convert crash date to Date format
    `CRASH TIME` = hm(`CRASH.TIME`),         # Convert crash time to Time format
    Hour = hour(`CRASH TIME`),               # Extract the hour of the crash
    Weekday = wday(`CRASH DATE`, label = TRUE),  # Extract the day of the week
    Month = month(`CRASH DATE`, label = TRUE),  # Extract the month
    Year = year(`CRASH DATE`),  # Extract the year
    BOROUGH = ifelse(is.na(BOROUGH), "UNKNOWN", BOROUGH) # Handle missing boroughs
  )

# Inspect the cleaned and transformed data
head(crash_data)
str(crash_data)

# Aggregate crash data by hour
hourly_crashes <- crash_data %>%
  group_by(Hour) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("NUMBER"), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# Visualize crashes by hour
ggplot(hourly_crashes, aes(x = Hour)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.INJURED, color = "Injured Persons"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.KILLED, color = "Killed Persons"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PEDESTRIANS.INJURED, color = "Injured Pedestrians"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PEDESTRIANS.KILLED, color = "Killed Pedestrians"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.CYCLIST.INJURED, color = "Injured Cyclists"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.CYCLIST.KILLED, color = "Killed Cyclists"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.MOTORIST.INJURED, color = "Injured Motorists"), size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.MOTORIST.KILLED, color = "Killed Motorists"), size = 1.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Injury and Fatality Trends by Hour of the Day", 
       x = "Hour of the Day", y = "Count of Injuries/Fatalities") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Injured Persons" = "steelblue", "Killed Persons" = "red", 
                                "Injured Pedestrians" = "darkorange", "Killed Pedestrians" = "darkred", 
                                "Injured Cyclists" = "seagreen", "Killed Cyclists" = "darkgreen", 
                                "Injured Motorists" = "purple", "Killed Motorists" = "darkviolet"))

# Aggregate crash data by weekday
weekday_crashes <- crash_data %>%
  group_by(Weekday) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("NUMBER"), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# Visualize injuries and fatalities by weekday
ggplot(weekday_crashes, aes(x = Weekday)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.INJURED), stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.KILLED), stat = "identity", fill = "red", alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Injuries and Fatalities by Weekday", x = "Weekday", y = "Count of Injuries/Fatalities")

# Aggregate crash data by month
monthly_crashes <- crash_data %>%
  group_by(Month) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("NUMBER"), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# Visualize injuries and fatalities by month
ggplot(monthly_crashes, aes(x = Month)) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.INJURED), stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_bar(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.KILLED), stat = "identity", fill = "red", alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Injuries and Fatalities by Month", x = "Month", y = "Count of Injuries/Fatalities")

# Aggregate crash data by year
yearly_crashes <- crash_data %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(across(starts_with("NUMBER"), sum, na.rm = TRUE))

# Visualize injury and fatality trends over years
ggplot(yearly_crashes, aes(x = Year)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.INJURED, color = "Injured Persons"), size = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(y = NUMBER.OF.PERSONS.KILLED, color = "Killed Persons"), size = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Injury and Fatality Trends by Year", x = "Year", y = "Count of Injuries/Fatalities") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Injured Persons" = "steelblue", "Killed Persons" = "red"))

```
